mechanism_narrative: A sustained reduction in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and reduces corporate borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns following 3-month FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0% and registers a month-over-month increase, accelerating labor market slack reduces household income expectations and corporate revenue forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 45% of triggered instances during NBER-dated recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising unemployment and declining industrial production capture a joint collapse in labor income and manufacturing output, amplifying recessionary earnings risk beyond the marginal effect of either series alone and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases month-over-month while INDPRO decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction term between UNRATE and INDPRO does not significantly improve out-of-sample 20-day return prediction vs. an additive model (F-test p > 0.05) over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During aggressive monetary easing cycles when the federal funds rate is below 2%, declines in industrial production signal a demand collapse that monetary stimulus cannot immediately offset, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered windows during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in retail sales signal weakening consumer spending and downward revisions to corporate revenue expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return following month-over-month RSAFS declines is not negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with falling retail sales indicates a synchronous contraction in manufacturing output and final demand, squeezing corporate margins through lower volumes and pricing power, which generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO decreases month-over-month while RSAFS decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The bivariate interaction of INDPRO and RSAFS changes produces a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in nonfarm payrolls confirm an entrenched labor market contraction that reduces aggregate wage income and consumption growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns follow more than 50% of trigger events over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,000 thousand units signal a deep housing recession that impairs financial sector balance sheets and construction employment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 45% of months when HOUST is below 1,000.
